name: SQL Injection Basics
description: Detect and exploit SQL injection vulnerabilities in a deliberately vulnerable web application (DVWA). Practice error-based, boolean-based, and UNION-based SQLi techniques in a fully isolated Docker environment.

flags.hint: The flag is in a table called 'flags'. Use a UNION SELECT to extract it from the id parameter on the product listing page.
Use ffuf or gobuster to enumerate hidden directories. The target has a non-standard path that isn't linked anywhere.
Check the Server response header. The version number is the key. Look up the CVE for that exact Apache version.

learning_objectives: Identify SQL injection-vulnerable input fields using single quote tests
Extract data using UNION-based SQL injection
Understand the difference between error-based, boolean-based, and time-based blind SQLi
Use sqlmap to automate SQLi detection and exploitation
Perform web directory enumeration using ffuf
Identify information disclosure via HTTP response headers
Understand parameterized queries as the correct remediation
